conjecture: The covering radius of the Eisenstein lattice is exactly 1 over square root 3
decomposition_raw: [{"id": "S1", "statement": "For 10,000 randomly selected points in the Euclidean plane, the maximum Euclidean distance from each point to its snapped Eisenstein lattice point is at most $1/\sqrt{3}$", "verifier": "covering_radius", "args": {"N": 10000}, "reason": "Establishes the upper bound on the Eisenstein lattice covering radius"}, {"id": "S2", "statement": "The point $(1/2, 1/(2\sqrt{3}))$ has Euclidean distance exactly $1/\sqrt{3}$ to the nearest Eisenstein lattice point, and the snap function correctly maps this point to that nearest lattice point", "verifier": "hex_closest_pack", "args": {"test_points": [[0.5, 1/(2 * 3**0.5)]]}, "reason": "Establishes the lower bound on the Eisenstein lattice covering radius"}]

sub_conjectures.statement: For 10,000 randomly selected points in the Euclidean plane, the maximum Euclidean distance from each point to its snapped Eisenstein lattice point is at most $1/{3}$
sub_conjectures.reason: Establishes the upper bound on the Eisenstein lattice covering radius
